title: rubus × extensifolius
intro: Rubus × extensifolius, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid of the wild raspberry and the black raspberry, characterized by its distinctive thimble-shaped fruit. The medicinal properties of Rubus × extensifolius include its high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are beneficial for reducing inflammation and improving overall health. The health benefits of consuming Rubus × extensifolius include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and provide protection against certain types of cancer. The herbal preparations of Rubus × extensifolius include jams, jellies, and teas, which can be made by infusing the fruit in hot water or combining it with other herbs and spices.
